evals.prompt: Build a Sirv product gallery with two zoomable images, a 360 spin, and a demo video. It appears above the fold on a product detail page.
evals.expected_output: SMV markup with one Sirv JS load, preconnect hints, a class="Sirv" viewer, child data-src items with data-type/data-alt, above-fold autostart handling, reserved layout space, and verification of thumbnails, zoom, spin, video, LCP, and CLS.
expectations: Uses Sirv Media Viewer instead of hand-building the gallery
Uses data-src child items for images, spin, and video
Adds meaningful data-alt or explicitly verifies Sirv file descriptions
Treats above-fold autostart and layout stability as performance concerns

evals.prompt: A smart Sirv .view gallery loads slowly and breaks a strict CSP in production. Audit the likely causes and patch the integration.
evals.expected_output: An evidence-first SMV debug workflow that checks the .view URL, script placement/module choice, network/CSP console errors, documented CSP directives, autostart behavior, and browser verification.
expectations: Recognizes .view as a Sirv smart gallery
Checks official Sirv CSP requirements before changing policy
Separates script loading, media loading, stats/video domains, and CSP causes
Does not replace the smart gallery with static image markup unless justified
Includes browser verification of gallery and network behavior

evals.prompt: A configurator needs custom buttons to switch product color groups in a Sirv gallery and report spin/zoom interactions to analytics.
evals.expected_output: A viewer API/events solution using Sirv.getInstance or Sirv.viewer.getInstance, data-id/data-group, enableGroup/disableGroup/switchGroup, Sirv.on or sirv:* DOM events, and focused browser tests.
expectations: Uses data-group or data-id for configurable assets
Handles viewer readiness before calling custom controls
Keeps analytics hooks separate from core viewer initialization
Verifies behavior in a real browser
